# Epic Decomposition

Break the design doc at `$ARGUMENTS` into subtask tickets with full requirement traceability.

Core principle: every requirement ID from the design doc appears in exactly one subtask checklist. Nothing dropped, nothing duplicated.

## Inputs

- Design doc on disk
- Parent epic ticket ID
- Adapter ID style: auth-task, AUTH-42, 5c4 - tkt identifiers or unambiguous partial IDs

## Process

1. Read the Requirements Summary section and build the complete requirement list.
2. Identify one-PR subtask boundaries that are independently mergeable and testable.
3. Build a dependency DAG between subtasks.
4. Draft each subtask body with parent epic, description, requirements, checklist, design reference, and dependencies.
5. Verify every requirement ID appears exactly once.
6. Present the proposed subtasks, dependency graph, and coverage matrix to the user for approval.
7. After approval, create the subtask tickets.

## Subtask Body Shape

```markdown
## Parent Epic
Part of <epic ticket link and title>

## Description
<What this PR delivers>

## Requirements
This subtask satisfies:
- REQ-ID: assertion

## Spec Compliance Checklist
- [ ] REQ-A-01: assertion
- [ ] REQ-A-02: assertion


## Design Reference
See `docs/plans/<design-doc>.md`.

## Dependencies
Formatter `dependency_annotation`:

```jinja
Depends on {{ ticket_ids | ticket_ids_pretty }}
```

Parameters:
- `ticket_ids`: `ticket_id_list` (list) - Non-empty list of adapter-specific ticket IDs.
```

## Create Subtask Tickets

For each approved subtask, write the body to a path matching `body_file_path`, then run:

Command `ticket_create`:
1. `argv`: `["tkt", "create", "<validated subtask title>", "--description", "<contents of validated body_file>"]` Read `body_file` after path validation and pass the full file contents as that argv element.

Before using values, normalize and validate each argument:

- `title`: `shell_safe_title` (string) - Free text for a CLI title argument. Allowed characters are ASCII letters, digits, spaces, and . , : ; ( ) / _ - @.
- `body_file`: `body_file_path` (string) - Relative path under .agent-skills-tmp/. No absolute paths, no .. segments, filename must start with an alphanumeric and end in .md.

Run each argv array directly through the runtime's structured command mechanism. Preserve one argv element per rendered token.

## Handoff

Report the created subtask tickets, the dependency order, and the coverage verification result. Suggest running `adversarial-design-review` on the decomposition before planning the first unblocked subtask.
